meta.title: Create VM: System Linux | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: Create Linux virtual machines on Proxmox VE with ProxMenux. Covers the curated official ISO catalogue (Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, Rocky, Mint, openSUSE, Alpine, Kali, Manjaro), the unified storage plan, optional GPU passthrough and post-install tips.
meta.ogTitle: Create VM: System Linux | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.ogDescription: Create Linux VMs on Proxmox VE with ProxMenux. Curated ISO catalogue, unified storage plan, optional GPU passthrough and post-install tips.

header.title: Create VM: System Linux
header.description: Create a Linux VM on Proxmox VE. ProxMenux ships with a curated catalogue of official Linux ISOs ready to download, plus a local-ISO picker, and routes everything through the shared VM wizard, the unified storage plan and the optional GPU passthrough assistant.

intro.title: What this script does
intro.body: The Linux selector offers two ProxMenux-managed sources of installation media: an official ISO from the curated list (auto-downloaded) or a local ISO you uploaded yourself. Whichever you pick, the rest of the flow is the shared wizard: CPU / RAM / BIOS → storage plan → optional GPU passthrough → VM creation.

config.heading: Default vs Advanced configuration
config.intro: After the ISO is selected, ProxMenux asks whether to use the default VM profile or open the advanced wizard.
config.advancedIntro: The advanced wizard exposes every parameter individually:

storagePlan.heading: Storage plan
storagePlan.body: After CPU / RAM, ProxMenux opens the Storage Plan menu, where you can combine virtual disks, imported disks and PCI passthrough devices in the same VM, adding items one at a time and finishing when you are done.
virtualDiskItems: Lists the Proxmox storage volumes available on the host.
You pick the target storage and the size in GB.
The disk is attached to the VM as SATA (sata0, sata1, … up to 6).
importDiskItems: Detects physical disks that are safe to import (system and protected disks are hidden).
You select one or more disks via a checklist.
Imported disks are attached as SATA via qm set.
storagePlan.pciTitle: c. Add Controller or NVMe (PCI passthrough)
pciItems: Detects SATA/SAS HBAs and NVMe devices on the host and lets you pass an entire controller into the VM. IOMMU must be enabled; the script offers to enable it and reboots if required.
A confirmation step warns about controller-wide risk (the whole card, including every disk on it, leaves the host).
The selected PCI devices are attached via qm set hostpciN: ….
storagePlan.resetBody: The menu also offers r (reset the current selection and start over) and d (finish and continue). You cannot finish with an empty plan.

gpu.heading: Optional GPU passthrough
gpu.body: After the storage plan, if a compatible GPU is detected on the host ProxMenux offers to launch the GPU passthrough assistant. See <gpuLink>Add GPU to a VM (Passthrough)</gpuLink> for how the assistant works — a host reboot may be required.

autoFeatures.heading: Automatic VM features
autoFeatures.efiBody: When OVMF (UEFI) is selected, ProxMenux creates a 4 MB EFI system disk on the storage you pick so the firmware has a place to store its variables.
autoFeatures.isoBody: The installation ISO is attached to ide2 as a CD-ROM, ready for first boot.
autoFeatures.guestBody: The guest agent channel is enabled in the VM config. You still need to install the agent inside the guest — see the tips below.

installOptions.heading: Linux installation options
installOptions.officialBody: ProxMenux ships with a curated list of official Linux ISOs. Pick one and the script downloads it straight from the upstream mirror into /var/lib/vz/template/iso, then attaches it to the VM.
installOptions.localBody: Uses any Linux ISO already present in /var/lib/vz/template/iso. The script lists every *.iso it finds and lets you pick one.

endToEnd.heading: What the script does end-to-end
items: Obtains the Linux ISO (official download or local file).
Applies the default or advanced CPU / RAM / BIOS / machine-type configuration.
Opens the storage plan and attaches the selected virtual disks, imported disks and PCI devices.
Creates the EFI disk when UEFI is selected.
Sets the boot order (disk first, then ISO).
Enables the QEMU Guest Agent channel.
Optionally runs the GPU passthrough assistant.
Generates a styled HTML description attached to the VM.
Starts the VM if you chose to.

postInstall.heading: Post-install tips
postInstall.guestAgentHeading: Install the QEMU Guest Agent inside the VM
postInstall.guestAgentBody: ProxMenux enables the guest agent channel in the VM config, but the agent itself has to be installed inside the guest. It enables graceful shutdown, filesystem freeze for consistent snapshots and accurate memory reporting in the Proxmox UI.
postInstall.virtioBody: Modern Linux kernels (2.6.25+) include VirtIO disk and network drivers out of the box, so you can switch the disk interface to virtio or virtio-scsi in the Advanced wizard and the network adapter to virtio without any extra driver step.
postInstall.virtioWarnBody: If you are installing something predating the 2.6.25 kernel, VirtIO modules may not be available at install time. In that case keep SATA / SCSI or provide a driver disk.
postInstall.trimHeading: Enable TRIM / discard on SSD-backed storage
postInstall.trimBody: When the VM sits on SSD-backed Proxmox storage and the disk was attached with the discard flag, you can forward trim commands from inside the guest. Two options:
trimItems: Run fstrim -av periodically (or enable the fstrim.timer unit on systemd distributions).
Or mount filesystems with the discard option in /etc/fstab.
postInstall.balloonBody: The virtio_balloon driver ships with modern Linux kernels. Proxmox uses it to reclaim unused memory from the VM and show accurate utilisation in the UI — no guest-side configuration required.

items.label: Import Disk Image to VM
items.tail: — attach an existing Linux .qcow2 / .img / .raw to a fresh VM.

items.label: Import VM from OVA / OVF
items.tail: — bring in a Linux VM exported from VMware / VirtualBox / Proxmox.

items.label: Add GPU to VM (Passthrough)
items.tail: — for Linux VMs that need GPU acceleration.
